name: PAI Indicator Calculation Methods
description: Detailed calculation methodologies for the 18 mandatory Principal Adverse Impact (PAI) indicators under SFDR Delegated Regulation (EU) 2022/1288, Annex I, Table 1. These methods are based on the Regulatory Technical Standards (RTS) and regulatory guidance from the European Supervisory Authorities (ESAs).
legalBasis: Commission Delegated Regulation (EU) 2022/1288, Annex I, Table 1

generalNotes.attributionFactor: For investee companies: attribution_factor_i = current_value_of_investment_i / enterprise_value_including_cash_i. This represents the share of the investee's total impact attributable to the financial market participant.
generalNotes.enterpriseValue: Enterprise value including cash = market capitalisation of equity at fiscal year end + total debt (book value) including current and non-current debt. For unlisted companies, use book value of equity + total debt.
generalNotes.currentValue: The current value of the investment as at the reporting reference date (31 December).
generalNotes.weightingApproach: Where weighted average is used: weight_i = current_value_of_investment_i / current_value_of_all_investments (portfolio weight).
generalNotes.referenceDate: 31 December of the reporting year. Where investee data is not available at that date, use most recent available data.
generalNotes.dataGaps: Where investee company data is not available, the financial market participant should use best efforts to obtain the data, including estimations. Where data is estimated, disclose this and describe the methodology used.

indicators.name: GHG emissions
indicators.category: Climate and other environment-related indicators — Greenhouse gas emissions

subIndicators.name: Scope 1 GHG emissions

inputs.name: current_value_investment_i
inputs.description: Current value of the financial market participant's investment in investee company i

inputs.name: enterprise_value_i
inputs.description: Enterprise value including cash of investee company i

inputs.name: Scope1_emissions_i
inputs.description: Total Scope 1 GHG emissions of investee company i (direct emissions from owned/controlled sources)

subIndicators.methodology: GHG Protocol Corporate Standard. Scope 1 includes all direct GHG emissions from sources owned or controlled by the company (e.g., combustion in owned boilers, furnaces, vehicles, chemical production, fugitive emissions).
subIndicators.aggregation: Sum of attributed emissions across all investee companies in the portfolio.
subIndicators.benchmark: No regulatory threshold. Compare against sector benchmarks and previous periods.

subIndicators.name: Scope 2 GHG emissions

inputs.name: current_value_investment_i
inputs.description: Current value of investment in investee company i

inputs.name: enterprise_value_i
inputs.description: Enterprise value including cash of investee company i

inputs.name: Scope2_emissions_i
inputs.description: Total Scope 2 GHG emissions of investee company i (indirect emissions from purchased electricity, steam, heating, cooling)

subIndicators.methodology: GHG Protocol Corporate Standard. Scope 2 includes indirect GHG emissions from the generation of purchased electricity, steam, heating and cooling consumed by the company. Location-based method required; market-based method may be reported additionally.
subIndicators.aggregation: Sum of attributed emissions across all investee companies in the portfolio.

subIndicators.name: Scope 3 GHG emissions

inputs.name: current_value_investment_i
inputs.description: Current value of investment in investee company i

inputs.name: enterprise_value_i
inputs.description: Enterprise value including cash of investee company i

inputs.name: Scope3_emissions_i
inputs.description: Total Scope 3 GHG emissions of investee company i (all other indirect emissions in value chain)

subIndicators.methodology: GHG Protocol Corporate Value Chain (Scope 3) Standard. Includes 15 categories of upstream and downstream emissions. Required from first reference period starting on or after 1 January 2023.
subIndicators.aggregation: Sum of attributed emissions across all investee companies in the portfolio.
subIndicators.notes: Scope 3 data quality is typically lower. Estimations and industry averages are commonly used. The RTS requires best efforts to obtain data.

subIndicators.name: Total GHG emissions
subIndicators.methodology: Sum of portfolio-level Scope 1, 2 and 3 emissions.

indicators.name: Carbon footprint
indicators.category: Climate and other environment-related indicators — Greenhouse gas emissions
indicators.unit: tonnes CO2e per EUR million invested

inputs.name: Portfolio_Total_GHG
inputs.description: Total GHG emissions attributed to the portfolio (PAI 1)

inputs.name: current_value_of_all_investments
inputs.description: Total current market value of all investments in the portfolio

indicators.methodology: Normalise the portfolio's total attributed GHG emissions by the total market value of the portfolio expressed in millions of EUR. This gives a per-unit-invested emissions metric, enabling comparison across portfolios of different sizes.
indicators.benchmark: No regulatory threshold. Industry average for equity portfolios approximately 50-150 tCO2e/EUR million. Paris-aligned benchmarks target significant reduction over time.

indicators.name: GHG intensity of investee companies
indicators.category: Climate and other environment-related indicators — Greenhouse gas emissions
indicators.unit: tonnes CO2e per EUR million revenue

inputs.name: current_value_investment_i
inputs.description: Current value of investment in investee company i

inputs.name: current_value_all_investments
inputs.description: Total portfolio value

inputs.name: Total_GHG_emissions_i
inputs.description: Total GHG emissions (Scope 1+2+3) of investee company i

inputs.name: revenue_i
inputs.description: Total revenue of investee company i

indicators.methodology: Weighted average of investee companies' GHG intensity (emissions per unit revenue), where weights are portfolio weights. This measures how carbon-efficient the investee companies' revenue generation is.
indicators.benchmark: Varies significantly by sector. High-carbon sectors (utilities, materials, energy) typically 100-5000+ tCO2e/EUR million revenue. Low-carbon sectors (technology, financials) typically < 50 tCO2e/EUR million revenue.

indicators.name: Exposure to companies active in the fossil fuel sector
indicators.category: Climate and other environment-related indicators — Greenhouse gas emissions

inputs.name: current_value_of_investments_in_fossil_fuel_companies
inputs.description: Sum of current values of investments in companies that derive any revenue from exploration, mining, extraction, production, processing, storage, refining or distribution of fossil fuels

inputs.name: current_value_of_all_investments
inputs.description: Total portfolio value

indicators.methodology: Binary classification: a company is counted as 'active in fossil fuels' if it derives ANY revenue from fossil fuel activities as defined in the RTS (hard coal/lignite, liquid fossil fuels, gaseous fossil fuels, electricity generation from fossil fuels). No materiality threshold — any involvement triggers inclusion.
fossilFuelDefinition: Exploration, mining, extraction, production, processing, storage, refining or distribution of hard coal and lignite
Exploration, extraction, production, processing, storage, refining or distribution of liquid fossil fuels (oil)
Exploration, extraction, manufacturing, processing, storage, refining or distribution of gaseous fossil fuels (natural gas)
Generation of electricity from fossil fuel sources
indicators.benchmark: Paris Aligned Benchmark (PAB): maximum 0% revenue from coal, 10% from oil, 50% from gas. Climate Transition Benchmark (CTB): less restrictive.

indicators.name: Share of non-renewable energy consumption and production
indicators.category: Climate and other environment-related indicators — Greenhouse gas emissions

inputs.name: weight_i
inputs.description: Portfolio weight: current_value_investment_i / current_value_all_investments

inputs.name: NR_consumption_i
inputs.description: Non-renewable energy consumed by investee company i

inputs.name: NR_production_i
inputs.description: Non-renewable energy produced by investee company i

inputs.name: total_consumption_i
inputs.description: Total energy consumed (renewable + non-renewable) by investee company i

inputs.name: total_production_i
inputs.description: Total energy produced (renewable + non-renewable) by investee company i

indicators.methodology: Weighted average of each investee company's non-renewable energy share. Non-renewable sources include coal, oil, natural gas, nuclear. Renewable sources include solar, wind, hydro, geothermal, biomass (sustainable).
indicators.benchmark: Global average non-renewable share approximately 80% (2023). EU 2030 target: at least 42.5% renewables.

indicators.name: Energy consumption intensity per high impact climate sector
indicators.category: Climate and other environment-related indicators — Greenhouse gas emissions
indicators.formula: For each NACE sector s: Energy_intensity_s = SUM_i_in_s( weight_i_in_s * (energy_consumption_i / revenue_EUR_millions_i) )
indicators.unit: GWh per EUR million revenue, per NACE sector

inputs.name: energy_consumption_i
inputs.description: Total energy consumption of investee company i

inputs.name: revenue_i
inputs.description: Total revenue of investee company i

inputs.name: NACE_sector_i
inputs.description: Primary NACE sector classification of investee company i

inputs.name: weight_i_in_s
inputs.description: Portfolio weight within the specific NACE sector

highImpactSectors.name: Electricity, gas, steam and air conditioning supply
Water supply; sewerage, waste management and remediation
Wholesale and retail trade; repair of motor vehicles

indicators.methodology: Calculated separately for each high impact sector (NACE A through H, and L). For each sector, compute the weighted average energy consumption intensity. Companies not in high impact sectors are excluded from this indicator.
indicators.aggregation: Sector-specific weighted averages. Report one figure per applicable NACE sector.
indicators.benchmark: Varies widely by sector. Compare against sector-specific benchmarks from EU ETS or industry associations.

indicators.name: Activities negatively affecting biodiversity-sensitive areas
indicators.category: Climate and other environment-related indicators — Biodiversity

inputs.name: current_value_investments_negatively_affecting
inputs.description: Sum of current values of investments in companies with sites/operations located in or near biodiversity-sensitive areas where their activities negatively affect those areas

inputs.name: current_value_of_all_investments
inputs.description: Total portfolio value

indicators.methodology: Two-step assessment: (1) Identify investee companies with sites/operations in or near biodiversity-sensitive areas (Natura 2000, UNESCO World Heritage Sites, Key Biodiversity Areas, etc.). (2) Of those, identify companies whose activities negatively affect those areas. The share is based on current investment values.
biodiversitySensitiveAreas: Key Biodiversity Areas (KBAs) as identified by IUCN
Other areas included under the Convention on Biological Diversity
indicators.benchmark: No regulatory threshold. Lower is better. Report qualitative description of affected areas.

indicators.name: Emissions to water
indicators.category: Climate and other environment-related indicators — Water

inputs.name: current_value_investment_i
inputs.description: Current value of investment in investee company i

inputs.name: enterprise_value_i
inputs.description: Enterprise value including cash of investee company i

inputs.name: emissions_to_water_i
inputs.description: Total emissions to water by investee company i, covering priority substances under the Water Framework Directive

inputs.name: current_value_all_investments
inputs.description: Total portfolio value

indicators.methodology: Attributed total emissions to water, normalised by portfolio value. Emissions to water include releases of priority substances as listed in the EU Water Framework Directive (Directive 2000/60/EC) and its daughter directives, including heavy metals, pesticides, and other hazardous substances.
indicators.aggregation: Sum of attributed emissions, normalised per EUR million invested.
indicators.benchmark: No regulatory threshold. Sector-specific — manufacturing, mining and agriculture sectors typically have higher emissions to water.

indicators.name: Hazardous waste and radioactive waste ratio
indicators.category: Climate and other environment-related indicators — Waste

inputs.name: current_value_investment_i
inputs.description: Current value of investment in investee company i

inputs.name: enterprise_value_i
inputs.description: Enterprise value including cash of investee company i

inputs.name: hazardous_waste_i
inputs.description: Total hazardous waste generated by investee company i as defined under Directive 2008/98/EC

inputs.name: radioactive_waste_i
inputs.description: Total radioactive waste generated by investee company i

inputs.name: current_value_all_investments
inputs.description: Total portfolio value

indicators.methodology: Attributed total hazardous and radioactive waste, normalised by portfolio value. Hazardous waste is defined per EU Waste Framework Directive (2008/98/EC) and the European List of Waste. Radioactive waste as defined under Council Directive 2011/70/Euratom.
indicators.aggregation: Sum of attributed waste, normalised per EUR million invested.
indicators.benchmark: No regulatory threshold. Manufacturing, chemical, mining, and energy (nuclear) sectors typically produce more hazardous/radioactive waste.

indicators.name: Violations of UN Global Compact principles and OECD Guidelines for Multinational Enterprises
indicators.category: Social and employee, respect for human rights, anti-corruption and anti-bribery matters

inputs.name: current_value_investments_in_violating_companies
inputs.description: Sum of current values of investments in companies that have been involved in violations of the UNGC principles or OECD Guidelines

inputs.name: current_value_of_all_investments
inputs.description: Total portfolio value

indicators.methodology: Binary assessment per investee company: has the company been involved in violations of the UN Global Compact principles (human rights, labour, environment, anti-corruption) or the OECD Guidelines for Multinational Enterprises? Sources include OECD National Contact Point complaints, UN reports, credible news sources, and ESG data providers. Assessment covers the current reporting period.
ungcPrinciples: Principle 1: Support and respect protection of internationally proclaimed human rights
Principle 2: Make sure not complicit in human rights abuses
Principle 3: Freedom of association and right to collective bargaining
Principle 4: Elimination of forced and compulsory labour
Principle 5: Effective abolition of child labour
Principle 6: Elimination of discrimination in employment
Principle 7: Support a precautionary approach to environmental challenges
Principle 8: Undertake initiatives to promote greater environmental responsibility
Principle 9: Encourage development of environmentally friendly technologies
Principle 10: Work against corruption including extortion and bribery
indicators.benchmark: Target: 0%. Any exposure indicates risk. Some exclusion policies use 0% tolerance.

indicators.name: Lack of processes and compliance mechanisms to monitor compliance with UN Global Compact principles and OECD Guidelines
indicators.category: Social and employee, respect for human rights, anti-corruption and anti-bribery matters

inputs.name: current_value_investments_without_compliance
inputs.description: Sum of current values of investments in companies without policies to monitor compliance with UNGC/OECD or grievance mechanisms

inputs.name: current_value_of_all_investments
inputs.description: Total portfolio value

indicators.methodology: Binary assessment: does the investee company have (1) policies/processes to monitor compliance with the UNGC principles and OECD Guidelines, AND (2) grievance/complaints handling mechanisms to address violations? Companies lacking either are counted. Assessment based on company disclosures, sustainability reports, and ESG data providers.
indicators.benchmark: Lower is better. Large-cap companies typically have compliance processes in place; SMEs may not. Engagement target for companies without.

indicators.name: Unadjusted gender pay gap
indicators.category: Social and employee, respect for human rights, anti-corruption and anti-bribery matters

inputs.name: weight_i
inputs.description: Portfolio weight of investee company i

inputs.name: avg_male_earnings_i
inputs.description: Average gross hourly earnings of male employees of investee company i

inputs.name: avg_female_earnings_i
inputs.description: Average gross hourly earnings of female employees of investee company i

indicators.methodology: Weighted average of investee companies' unadjusted gender pay gaps. The unadjusted gender pay gap is defined as the difference between average gross hourly earnings of male and female paid employees as a percentage of male gross earnings. 'Unadjusted' means no adjustment for differences in individual characteristics (education, experience, sector, etc.). Definition aligns with Eurostat methodology.
indicators.benchmark: EU average gender pay gap approximately 12-13% (2022). Target: lower is better, with 0% representing full parity. EU Pay Transparency Directive requires action if gap > 5%.

indicators.name: Board gender diversity
indicators.category: Social and employee, respect for human rights, anti-corruption and anti-bribery matters

inputs.name: weight_i
inputs.description: Portfolio weight of investee company i

inputs.name: female_board_members_i
inputs.description: Number of female board members of investee company i

inputs.name: total_board_members_i
inputs.description: Total number of board members of investee company i

indicators.methodology: Weighted average of the ratio of female to total board members across investee companies. 'Board members' includes all members of the administrative, management and supervisory bodies (depending on governance structure — one-tier or two-tier board).
indicators.benchmark: EU Women on Boards Directive: 40% of non-executive directors or 33% of all directors by June 2026. EU average approximately 33% (2023). Best practice: at least 40%.

indicators.name: Exposure to controversial weapons
indicators.category: Social and employee, respect for human rights, anti-corruption and anti-bribery matters

inputs.name: current_value_investments_in_controversial_weapons
inputs.description: Sum of current values of investments in companies involved in manufacture or sale of controversial weapons

inputs.name: current_value_of_all_investments
inputs.description: Total portfolio value

indicators.methodology: Binary assessment: is the investee company involved in the manufacture or selling of controversial weapons? Controversial weapons are defined as: anti-personnel mines (Ottawa Treaty), cluster munitions (Convention on Cluster Munitions), chemical weapons (Chemical Weapons Convention), biological weapons (Biological Weapons Convention). Involvement includes direct manufacture, components specifically designed for these weapons, and trading/distribution.

weaponDefinitions.antiPersonnelMines: Banned under the 1997 Ottawa Treaty (Mine Ban Treaty). Mines designed to be detonated by the presence, proximity or contact of a person.
weaponDefinitions.clusterMunitions: Banned under the 2008 Convention on Cluster Munitions. Munitions designed to disperse or release explosive submunitions.
weaponDefinitions.chemicalWeapons: Banned under the 1993 Chemical Weapons Convention. Toxic chemicals and their precursors intended for use as weapons.
weaponDefinitions.biologicalWeapons: Banned under the 1972 Biological Weapons Convention. Microbial or other biological agents or toxins for hostile purposes.

indicators.benchmark: Target: 0%. Most responsible investment policies mandate complete exclusion. EU PAB and CTB regulations require 0% exposure.

indicators.name: GHG intensity
indicators.category: Indicators applicable to investments in sovereigns and supranationals — Environmental
indicators.unit: tonnes CO2e per EUR million GDP

inputs.name: weight_i
inputs.description: Portfolio weight of sovereign/supranational investment i: current_value_sovereign_investment_i / current_value_all_sovereign_investments

inputs.name: country_GHG_emissions_i
inputs.description: Total GHG emissions of the country/region

inputs.name: country_GDP_i
inputs.description: Gross Domestic Product of the country/region

indicators.methodology: Weighted average of the GHG intensity (emissions per unit GDP) of sovereign/supranational issuers in the portfolio. GHG emissions data typically sourced from UNFCCC national inventories, World Bank, or IEA. GDP data from IMF, World Bank, or Eurostat.
indicators.benchmark: Global average approximately 400-500 tCO2e/EUR million GDP. EU average approximately 200-300 tCO2e/EUR million GDP. Significant variation by country — fossil-fuel dependent economies much higher.

indicators.name: Investee countries subject to social violations
indicators.category: Indicators applicable to investments in sovereigns and supranationals — Social

inputs.name: investee_country_list
inputs.description: List of all countries in which the portfolio holds sovereign/supranational investments

inputs.name: country_social_violation_flag
inputs.description: Whether the country is subject to social violations as referred to in international treaties, UN principles, and national law

indicators.methodology: Count the number of investee countries subject to social violations. Social violations are assessed against international treaties and conventions, UN principles and, where applicable, national law. Sources include UN Human Rights Council reports, Freedom House, ILO supervisory bodies, and EU sanctions lists.
socialViolationSources: International treaties on human rights and civil liberties
UN Human Rights Council Universal Periodic Review findings
ILO Committee of Experts on the Application of Conventions
EU restrictive measures (sanctions) for human rights violations
indicators.aggregation: Count and percentage of investee countries.
indicators.benchmark: Target: 0 countries. Some investors apply sovereign exclusion lists based on severe human rights violations.

indicators.name: Exposure to fossil fuels through real estate assets
indicators.category: Indicators applicable to investments in real estate assets — Environmental

inputs.name: value_of_RE_assets_involved_in_fossil_fuels
inputs.description: Value of real estate assets involved in extraction, storage, transport or manufacture of fossil fuels

inputs.name: current_value_of_all_RE_investments
inputs.description: Total value of all real estate investments in the portfolio

indicators.methodology: Share of real estate investments that are involved in fossil fuel activities. A real estate asset is considered 'involved' if it is used for the extraction, storage, transport or manufacture of fossil fuels. This includes properties such as oil storage facilities, coal terminals, gas processing plants, and similar industrial real estate.
indicators.aggregation: Simple share of real estate portfolio value.

indicators.name: Exposure to energy-inefficient real estate assets
indicators.category: Indicators applicable to investments in real estate assets — Environmental

inputs.name: value_of_energy_inefficient_RE_assets
inputs.description: Value of real estate assets with EPC rating below C (or in the top 30% of energy consumption for non-EPC jurisdictions)

inputs.name: current_value_of_all_RE_investments
inputs.description: Total value of all real estate investments in the portfolio

indicators.methodology: Share of real estate investments in energy-inefficient assets. Energy-inefficient is defined as: (a) where Energy Performance Certificates (EPCs) are available: buildings with EPC rating below class C; (b) where EPCs are not available: buildings in the top 30% of the national energy consumption distribution. The assessment should cover all directly held real estate assets.
indicators.epcThreshold: Below class C (i.e., class D, E, F, or G)
indicators.nonEpcFallback: Top 30% of national energy consumption distribution for the relevant building type and location
indicators.aggregation: Simple share of real estate portfolio value.
indicators.benchmark: EU Energy Performance of Buildings Directive (recast) aims for all buildings to be at least EPC E by 2030 and EPC D by 2033 (residential). Decarbonisation pathway suggests reducing this share over time.
